articles.title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
articles.description: Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless novels, screenplays, pieces of journalism, started McSweeney's, and founded multiple schools and nonprofits that support writers and the arts more broadly. So one might expect he'd roll into the company's offices and offer […]

articles.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
articles.description: TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra. The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells The Verge. YouTube has been working on a similar tool […]

articles.title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
articles.description: Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done. So what does Apple really want here, and why is it picking such a public fight with OpenAI? On this episode of The […]

articles.title: Meet Yang Zhilin, the CEO and founder behind China's buzzy new Kimi K3 AI model - Business Insider
articles.description: [www.businessinsider.com] Subscribe Newsletters AI Meet Yang Zhilin, the CEO and founder behind China's buzzy new Kimi K3 AI model By Lakshmi Varanasi You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email. Read in app Copy link Email Facebook WhatsApp X LinkedIn Bluesky Threads Impact Link Save Saved This story is available exclusively to Business Insider subscribers. Become an Insider and start reading now. Have an account? . There's a founder making waves in the AI industry that's not an American tech billionaire like Elon Musk, Sam Altman, or Dario Amodei. Yang Zhilin is th [www.businessinsider.com] The company plans to release its model weights by July 27, allowing developers to download, modify, and build on top of it. Like DeepSeek, another Chinese AI model that rattled Silicon Valley last year, Kimi K3 is fueling debate over whether China's more open approach to AI is narrowing the gap on the closed models offered by US tech companies. Here's why Kimi K3 has generated so much buzz. 1. It's powerful — especially at coding Kimi K3 has 2.8 trillion parameters — the internal values that help an AI model learn and generate responses — making it the largest open-weight AI model announced to [www.businessinsider.com] Here's what smart people in the worlds of tech and academia are saying about it. David Sacks, co-chair of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology David Sacks, the venture capitalist who served as President Donald Trump's first AI and crypto czar before moving in March to cochair the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, called the release "concerning." In a Friday X post sharing the Arena leaderboard, Sacks noted it was the first time a Chinese model had taken the top spot

articles.title: Open Weight Models Are Turning Inference Into A Control Point - Forbes
articles.description: [www.forbes.com] Model pricing explains much of the urgency. Artificial Analysis reported GPT-5.6 Sol at $1.04 per Intelligence Index task, roughly a third of what Claude Fable 5 costs for a score one point higher. Six labs now field a model above 50 on that index, up from two in early June. Cost per task is a benchmark figure rather than a customer price, and one index point hides real differences in coding, tool use and hallucination behavior. It supports the view that routing and portability gain value as the number of adequate models grows. The Challenges Ahead [...] What The Funding Does Not Prove I const [www.forbes.com] The lobbyists for the US frontier labs have a recipe for the US government to win: "You don’t need to 'ban open source' (one of the dumber motifs of AI policy discussion). You just need to direct every agency to issue soft law that creates FUD. 'A Federal Reserve Advisory Bulletin found that there may be backdoors in Chinese AI models.' It needn't be that well justified. You just create enough regulatory risk that every regulated enterprise backs off." — Dean Ball Except it's a recipe that only serves them, not the country. The actual response to Chinese competition in open weights is US open-

items.title: Meet Yang Zhilin, the CEO and founder behind China's buzzy new Kimi K3 AI model - Business Insider
items.summary: 月27日将发布Kimi K3模型权重，开发者可下载修改，该模型含2.8万亿参数。
items.why_it_matters: 开源策略或缩小中美AI差距，影响开发者生态与竞争格局。

items.title: Open Weight Models Are Turning Inference Into A Control Point - Forbes
items.summary: 多实验室模型超50分，成本降低推动路由价值，但监管风险可能阻碍开放。
items.why_it_matters: 定价变化改变推理控制点，监管压力或影响企业采用开放模型意愿。

items.title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
items.summary: 作家Dave Eggers向OpenAI员工表示ChatGPT正在“沉默整整一代人”。
items.why_it_matters: 引发对生成式AI内容同质化及文化影响的深层担忧。

items.title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
items.summary: Apple起诉OpenAI，指控其存在不当行为并寻求法律救济。
items.why_it_matters: 诉讼结果将影响两大科技巨头在AI领域的竞争态势与合作边界。

items.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
items.summary: TikTok在美国测试AI形象检测工具，创作者可主动举报疑似AI生成内容。
items.why_it_matters: 平台加强内容治理，推动行业建立AI生成标识标准以保护创作者权益。
